summary.intent: Add file-based routing to a React Native/Expo app and create a new CheckpointTimeline screen, then migrate the entire app from Expo to Electron for better web component library compatibility.
summary.outcome: Successfully migrated the gossamer app from React Native/Expo to Electron + Vite + React Router DOM, with all 6 screens and 6 components ported, a new CheckpointTimeline screen added, and the Electron dev server running.

repo: Project root is /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/app — an Expo app with screens in src/screens/ and components in src/components/
The app connects to an API server at localhost:3000 with WebSocket support for live updates
expo-router puts routes in an 'app/' directory by default, which clashes with the Expo project already being in an 'app/' folder — requires overriding the root dir in app.json
After Electron migration, new structure: main process at src/main/index.ts, preload at src/preload/index.ts, renderer at src/renderer/src/
electron-vite outputs to out/ by default, not dist-electron/ — package.json main field must point to out/main/index.js
React Native primitives (View, Text, TouchableOpacity, StyleSheet) were shimmed in primitives.tsx to make migration mechanical

code.finding: expo-router's root directory can be changed from 'app' to any name (e.g. 'routes') via the expo-router plugin config in app.json to avoid folder naming collisions
Shim file maps React Native primitives (View→div, Text→span, TouchableOpacity→button, StyleSheet.create→identity) enabling near-direct port of RN component code to HTML/React
Layout.tsx computes screen title from route path + location state, replacing expo-router's Stack.Screen options pattern
main field must be 'out/main/index.js' (electron-vite default output) not 'dist-electron/main/index.js'

workflow: When expo-router conflicts with project folder naming, set the router root in app.json plugin config rather than restructuring the project
For RN→web migrations, creating primitive shims first makes the subsequent component rewrites nearly mechanical
electron-vite build catches TypeScript errors without needing to run the full dev server — useful for validation before launching
Initial Electron dev run failure (exit code 1) was due to wrong main path in package.json, not a code error — always verify the main field matches build output directory

friction: expo-router's default 'app/' route directory collided with the existing Expo project directory, requiring a rename to 'routes/' and app.json config change
electron-vite outputs to 'out/' but package.json was initially set to 'dist-electron/', causing the first Electron launch to fail
Spurious background task failure notification from the first failed Electron run caused confusion even after the fix was applied
open_items: Old Expo/RN files in app/src/screens/ and app/src/components/ still exist alongside the new Electron renderer versions — cleanup may be needed
The routes/ directory (expo-router routes) is now orphaned after the Electron migration and should be deleted
No web component library has been added yet — the migration enables this but it was not implemented
MarkdownView was fully rewritten with semantic HTML but may need styling/CSS work to match original appearance
Checkpoint worktree warning from API server startup is pre-existing and unaddressed
